Series // [[Travelers 01|Travelers]] █


''Proceed >>''
{{Background|file=Path Way BG.png}} ='''Travelers'''= [[File:Traveler Sign.png|thumb|Traveler's group insignia.|320x320px|link=]] ==Description== TravelersThe name of this group is unofficial, and they do not appear to have one of their own. However, many still refer to them by this name regardless. are an advanced race of entities possessing powerful reality-bending properties. They are distinguishable by their lavish suits of armorIt's comparable to a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Powered_exoskeleton powered exoskeleton], a possible concept that provides enhanced performance for the wearer.. They consider themselves part of the "Outsiders," a group of beings originating from the aptly named "Outside," in a sense, they do not belong to the Backrooms. Having recently arrived, they are actively exploring various levels and interacting with many wanderers along the way. Unlike any typical entities, they are considered as "alien" wanderers and are often welcomed due to their properties when fending off hostile entities and navigating dangerous levels for the wanderers. It is believed that they are the architects of the [[The Palace in the Void|Palace]] and the manufacturers of the [[Golden Artifacts|Golden Artifacts]]. Though, those creations are attributed to their ancestors. They are the enemies of the [[Recovered Data File|Marauders]]. == Overview == This group of entities are remain unknown due to their recent arrival in the Backrooms and their infrequent encounters with wanderers. They are more of a research-oriented and peacekeeping group than a military organization despite their intimidating presence. However, they will defend themselves and others when threatened or provoked. === Structure === The group operates independently, lacking a formal command hierarchy. While the absence of structured leadership might seem disadvantageous, their strong sense of kinship and loyalty allows them to function cohesively. While they do not have any leader instead they have their respected figure in their kind referred to as the "Elder," acting as the representative of their group. Their social structure is somewhat technocratic, as they prefer innovation and discovery. They are known to create advanced anomalous objects such as the Golden Artifacts, make significant discoveries, and, according to some accounts, they even stabilize unstable and dangerous levels within the Backrooms. They are altruistic by nature, often seen assisting other wanderers to the best of their abilities when needed. === Members === The group appears to be small, as encounters with them are rare. Although they have established a few outposts, their total population remains undetermined. === Technology === While little is known about their technology, there is no doubt that it is highly advanced. The Golden Artifacts, for example, are crafted and utilized daily. These objects are their tools and some of which, according to some accounts, allows them to manipulate levels, such as stabilizing dangerous levels to make them safer. Do note, some pieces of their technology bears similiar to ours but they are far from conventional, and perhaps, abstract. ==== Equipment ==== They equip various ancient weapons' material made from gold, gem and/or marble while it has this nebulous-like materials that use in the sharp blades or blunt parts which to deal tremendously. While their firearms, functions and bears similarly to those of humans', but these similarities ends when they lack conventional features such as the lack ejection ports or propellants which it fires solid materials, and some of their firing systems are alien in nature such as carved marbled stone with engravings or a small serve as their ammunition. Their designs were lavish, almost artistic, while some of theirs weapons also featured anomalous properties to give tactical advantage for the wielders.Travelers are capable of equipping and operating conventional weapons. Interestingly, whenever they possess our weapons, they have an ability to enhance them in such various ways. The most defining feature of this group is the Matrix—a critical part within their suits. The Matrix consists of a column of spines adorned with gold and jewels, situated on their backs. This feature appears to be a source of their powerful reality-bending properties and perhaps for aesthetic purposes. === Objectives === Based on various citations and recordings, it is safe to assume that the Travelers' goals are benign and straightforward: to explore and conduct research within the Backrooms, particularly within their abandoned Palace, while assisting wanderers on their journeys, especially those in need. As enemies of the Marauders, they focus on preventing their invasion attempts, often attacking them first when necessary. This may serve as one of their primary objectives, as they work to protect both their interests and the safety of others within the Backrooms. == Bases & Outposts == Since their arrival, the Travelers have built their base within their starting level, unlike in Level 0. According to one of their members, it initially described as a scorching, unstable, and perpetually dark desert inhabited by hostile entities but it has undergone significant transformation which they managed to stabilize and reshape the environment. It transformed into a bright, cool desert with marble-white sands and patches of oases. === Base === Unlike their Void counterpart, their base resides within this transformed starting level. Although we have not yet traveled to this level, the Travelers have assured us that they have established "slip Palace" that connect their well-established colonies to this base. These Palace allow for efficient travel and communication between their various outposts. === Outposts === The Travelers have established several outposts across various levels. Regardless of hostile nature of some levels, they ensure their outposts are well-protected, using entities such as Keepers for security and other purposes. Each outpost typically serves research functions, although some are dedicated to specific tasks, and in rare cases, they are used for repairing unstable levels. While their outposts are located elsewhere away from discovered levels and their total number remains undetermined, there are some do have situated in ours but situated in a undiscovered locations. ==== Current List of Outposts ==== ====== [[Level 6|Level 6]] ====== The sole purpose of this outpost is to search for and rescue lost wanderers, who guide the wanderers who have lost their way in Level 6. It is operated by few Travelers. This outpost was originally established by the Red Cross and the Backroom Colonists, who requested the Travelers for assistance in rescuing their important members, they would later involve and help wanderers out of their diligence, and coincidentally, their interests of the level. ====== [[Level 11|Level 11]] ====== The purpose of their outpost is to research the nature of Level 11. However, They are on high alert due to reports of undiscovered entities migrating within this level and, on rare occasions, small Marauder forces attempting to establishing themselves in the level. Currently, four Travelers are stationed within this level, but others will often visit the outpost. Wanderers are welcome to stay and resupply. They have established an outpost in some apartment in some city, farthest from the entrance. They do placed their group's insignia enough for others to notice. ==Biology== The Travelers are humanoid entities, they standing roughly around 6'7" (200.66 cm) tall, though some individuals or variants may be taller or shorter. They can be either male or female, but their true biological characteristics remain unknown. There are speculations around these entities' biology and their suits. Some suggest they are mechanical-like entities, evident to their visible mechanical components within their suits and the properties that enable these parts to function. Visual analysis indicates that some exposed parts of their bodies appear to be made of spectacle colors of stars and space emanating within their bodies and intensifies whenever they use it which this has led some to believed that they are non-corporeal entities inhabiting their humanoid bodies. However, others believed that their nebula-like features are merely for aesthetic purposes, rather than actual physical traits much like their Matrix components. In contrast, others believe that the Travelers do have biological characteristics. They claimed that they are capable of shedding blood, which, when injured, appears dark and non-reflective while emanating stars and nebulae with a glint of gold, while in other cases, they shed red blood with also have glitter of gold, suggesting the possibility of blood types. The Travelers are remarkably agile, capable of scaling walls and running on foot without losing momentumDespite wearing cumbersome armor and weapons, they maintain a notable degree of agility.. They can leap distances with precision which allows them to maneuver and scales in difficult environments without difficulty while evade attacks that would typically be unavoidable. Their exceptional sense of balance enables them to stand on thin wires without faltering, and their sharp reflexes allow them to evade dangers and counterattack from seemingly impossible angles while maintaining remarkable accuracy. In addition, they demonstrate the extraordinary skill of deflecting projectiles when using hand weapons. They display superhuman traits such as exceptional strength, speed, and resilience. Moreover, individuals along with the variants will possess unique additional abilities, further diversifying their abilities. They appear to be highly resistant, if not entirely immune, to all forms of diseases and even anomalous effects. It is possible that their Matrix, believed to function as their power source, are central to their anomalous properties. These spines may be responsible enhance their abilities. === Reality-Warping Properties === Travelers perform a wide range of reality-warping properties to gain advantages and help themselves in various situations, often in combat. After reviewing with some Traveler, stated that each individual has an affinity for certain properties, which they use in conjunction with their suits, which referred to as their "Matrix Designation," to maximize their potential. The range of their reality-warping properties varies depending on the variants and the users. (For more details, see Variants) ==Behaviors / Culture== Regardless of their imposing appearances and their powerful reality-warping properties, it gives an intimidating impressions to wanderers which often misleads interactions in several scenarios. However, they exhibit human-like behavior, with distinct social norms and cultural practicesThis includes references to various media, as some of the entities make mention of them during recreational activities or in a context of humor. Although their knowledge of such media appears limited, it raises questions regarding the source of this information and how they obtained it.. Few interactions indicates they are mostly approachable. Overall, all of these demonstrate compassion, treating them all as equals, including entities such as Facelings. The Travelers are pacifists, preferring incapacitate over killing targets (excluding hostile entities). However, under extreme circumstances and/or exhausting their options, lethal force will be applied. Creating objects from nothing to something is their ancient tradition, often used for art and recreation, while they used this to hone their skills this also helps their properties. Some of the members do have academic backgrounds or expertise in their fields, which is often tied to their respective variants, allowing them to apply their knowledge efficiently. While their primary objective is exploration and study, they also provide protection to wanderers and entities. Contrary to their appearances, they are enjoy even simplest things, even in the Backrooms regardless of them referring to it as "garbage." They particularly enjoy accompanying others of their kind or other wanderers during their travels which can be seen socializing with them, even befriending them to some extent. Their practices include martial traditions and the use of military jargon, which bears resemblance to conventional military forces in the Frontrooms. While they possess personal names, many prefer to be addressed by their callsigns or nicknames, which they frequently use. Their callsigns are structured with: two Greek letters, numbers, and an alphanumeric identifiers (for example "Theta-Nu-456-A"). In combat, they prefer stealth and quick decisive attacks, using these tactics to quickly eliminate threats silently before the enemies could be alarmed. To those who have seen in their engagements, they describe their combat style similar to dancing, characterized by fluid movements and precision strikes. Unintentionally, this induces psychological distress in their enemies. They are adept in utilizing their properties while they are aware of their environments and exploit it to gain tactical advantages. With enough time to assess their surroundings, they effectively adapt and improvise, using their properties effectively in several high-stress situations. ==Variants== Similar to the Marauders, they distinct variants, referred to as "Matrix Designations," which are primarily identified by the unique designs of their suits of armor and the golden spines. Each variant possesses a specific set of reality-warping properties, with some variants provide roles in their teams or interacting Levels in the Backrooms. They already have designated codenames. It is important to note that each members that wore their suits are varied in appearance and even their properties to distinct themselves. Currently, seven variants have been documented and classified as follows:
|-|Variant A= == Astrals ==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Astral A.png|thumb|A male Astral holding weapons in each hand. The "heads" of the entities, whether they are their true faces or just a type of headwear with unique materials.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Astral B.png|thumb|A female Astral holding her rifle.|420x420px|link=]]
Donned in ornate, sleek suits of armor with several gold pieces on it but some others will have accessories. Their distinctive features include their helmets, or "heads," which reveal stars and nebulae that emit a vivid glow whenever these variants use their properties but some will have their helmets less exposed. Moreover, the glow extends to some parts of their bodies when drawing more power. Their spinal columns are simpler and more compact in design, glowing brightly and leaving a trail of light whenever these entities move or use their properties. ===Equipment=== Equipped with various firearms but they prefer over quiet ones either throwing knives, bows, or firearms with suppressors on, if they do not doing covertly they will do so if they wish. For hand weapons, they either equipped with sabres that resembles Japanese katanas or daggers equipped on each hands. ===Properties=== Their properties include movement and displacement by manifesting spatial rifts through which they can enter and exit instantly. They can manifest bright star-like orbs from their hands, which they later throw. Upon contact, these orbs explode into a cloud of gas similar to a supernova. These objects have strong gravitational forces that attract any type of attack and even hostiles if close enough. They can summon Void Cells in an inert state, which, once deployed, sink into the surface and then resurface to assist in combat. These Void Cells are diversed and linked to these variants, indicating their customized designs, whether for cosmetic or functional purposes. Finally, they can surround themselves with a blinding light that absorbs any luminous sources as well as any shadow or darkness cast upon it. These entities' bodies become covered in a spectacle of stars and nebulae, and they discharge powerful waves of energy that disintegrate any hostiles while sparing non-hostiles, indicating that their properties are highly accurate in discerning between friend and foe. |-|Variant B= ==Crusader==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Crusader A.png|thumb|A male Crusader wields a massive sword in proportion to its stature.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Crusader B.png|thumb|A female Crusader wielding her rapier. Behind her helmet emanating energy resembling long flowing hair, it is unknown whether if that is a part of their or merely an accessory.|420x420px|link=]]
Variants dressed in ornamental armored suits resemble knights from the medieval period, often wearing armor pieces while some tabards, or shoulder capes, or a long scarf, despite well-armored it does not sacrificing their agility. However, some these instances will be more bulkier while others can be seen wear less armor in favor for more agility. Their spinal column is more pronounced, and each segment are plated and armored. ===Equipment=== Crusaders are commonly armed with heavy two-handed close-combat weapons, preferably longswords, maces, and war hammers, and each of these weapons cause wide-ranged attacks every time they swung their weapon, allowing them to dispatch multiple enemies at once in every direction. They also equipped shields. These shields they equipped had different functions when they were on operations. They can equipped with ranged weapons like shotguns and other heavy weapons while they can use lighter firearms in tandem with their shields. ===Properties=== Their main properties are focused on wide-ranging devastation yet they are precise enough to not harm any of their non-hostile in their wake. They can be seen throwing a ball of blue flame at their target which cause any enemies to set ablaze. They will brace themselves, which during that state makes the Crusaders nearly invulnerable, but this makes these variants almost immovable, it seems they can absorb any harm then convert it into something beneficial for themselves, either for more firepower or for something else, such as making them reinvigorate themselves upon leaving their state. In rare cases, Crusaders will cover themselves in blue fire, emitting bright light, indicating of entering their more destructive state, which causes their weapons to emit and imbue with nebulae-like flame, this state will immediately disintegrate any enemies upon contact. |-|Variant C= ==Architect==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Architect A.png|thumb|A male Architect with his hammer while a floating object manifested from his hand.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Architect B.png|thumb|A female Architect with her hammer leaning on a white marble structure.|420x420px|link=]]
Architects wear their distinctive crescent-shaped helmets, denoting their position equivalent to our engineers. While not heavily armored, these variants have several gems and other geometrical objects attached to their suits, while others featuring several apertures instead. These objects are thought to be their catalysts or tools for their properties to function. Their spinal column, specifically the thoracic region between T1 and T7, is more pronounced. ===Equipment=== They prefer automatic firearms or, on occasion, heavy weapons to decimate enemies. The objects on their suits are used to assist Architects with various tasks, primarily building structures, and some believe they also create weapons. They can engaged in close combat, they use heavy close-combat weapons such as war hammers or axes, as well as shields. ===Properties=== Architects, as previously stated, rely on their objects, which act as catalysts for their properties. The most common of their anomalous properties are their marbled structures, used for both offense and defense. Commonly, they manifest small rectangular pillars in a square formation around their perimeter—possibly other instances of Void Cells. These pillars will emerge from the surface, manifesting a marble-like substance where they affix in its location temporarily. The pillars open their shells and fire projectiles at their targets, then submerge and re-emerge in different locations with sudden and sporadic movements, making them unpredictable. Their size and shapes of these pillars vary depending on the Architects' creations. They frequently manifest marbled plinths with a floating sphere of energy above the platform when an immediate supply is needed. The plinths have various uses, such as generating an anomalous field that rejuvenates anyone in close proximity back to full health. If they run out of supplies, they can replenish themselves, fellow Travelers, and others. They also throw gems with several intersecting rings that spin randomly, gradually spinning faster until they release a burst of energy that immobilizes any enemies within range, rendering them helpless. In some cases, the energy pulls enemies to the gem's location, grouping them for the Architect to take down. If necessary, they manifest a small orb from their hands and insert it into the ground, causing a marble substance to spread on the surrounding surface, eventually forming monolithic structures. These structures are adorned with a plethora of symbols and carvings and serve various functions depending on the Architect's desires. The most common function is firing a beam of light that disintegrates anything in its path. |-|Variant D= ==Psion==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Psion A.png|thumb|A male Psion holding a dagger while emitting its energy from its body.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Psion B.png|thumb|A female Psion using her properties that manifest floating crystalline objects assuming it was created from her pure psionic properties.|420x420px|link=]]
Their suits resembles of black coats, though some were white, with ornate tailcoats loosely dangling down from their suits, and their helmets have notable features such as halo-like accessories along with numbers of spikes on it, giving them an overall intimidating presence. Their spinal column, specifically C1 and C2 in the cervical region, are more pronounced and their spinous processes are extended. ===Equipment=== They were mostly armed with sidearms, short swords, and daggers, or bladed scepters. They can be seen using their unconventional weapons that has variety of functions on each weapons. ===Properties=== Psions possesses powerful psionic properties that emanating this field of energy from their bodies and it will intensify when using. They have a variety of psychic-related properties that Psions possess, however they will limit themselves for several reasons unless under extreme circumstances. Their common use are inducing mass hallucinations on their enemies, confusing them and creates chaos in the process, some can able to manifest this ghostly images, either the copies of this entities or anyone, which use them either as decoys or assist them in combat. There is other instance of this variants by materializing floating gems which use to chase down their enemies, perforating them with extreme velocity or anomalous ball of flame that does . As mentioned, they have reason to limit themselves but if they deemed to allow themselves to use their other powerful psionic properties, they can alter their surroundings in a localized area with various effects such as manifesting this golden chains that upon contact cause the enemies' bodies to rupture with spikes or some causing those chains to forcefully bind them, rendering them vulnerable. |-|Variant E= ==Martyr==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Martyr A.png|thumb|A male Martyr surrounded by nebulous flame.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Martyr B.png|thumb|A female Martyr surrounded by nebulous flame.|420x420px|link=]]
They wore white suits with ornate tailcoats, or some wore capes or robes, and their prominent feature of these variants are their chest area emitted starlight or sometimes emanating aurora, it is said their ornamented helmets with golden ring accessories and their suits signifies their position, that being as physicians or sometimes scientists from different branch of science. Their spinal columns are minimalistic and compact, with only segments of glowing pieces and a few gold pieces around their backs. As Martyrs, it is their duty to treat the wounded and assist them whenever they came across. Most wanderers often call these variants simply know as "doctors." ===Equipment=== They typically carried lighter weapons like sidearms and submachine guns but some used rifles and precision rifles. They can equip hand weapons such as dual-wielding batons, staves, and double-ended glaives. ===Properties=== Martyrs' properties are mainly focus on providing survivability of their friendlies while they can efficiently do so when operating in an middle of intense combat. However, their properties are capable for combat in a interesting way, such as these golden objects that emits flame they manifest which explode into fiery wave, it can harm only to their enemies but not the allies if they are within the same area, instead they able restore them to health They are also manifest these ornate diamond-shaped objects suspended above the head level, these objects provide invigorating effects for those in its vicinity while protecting them from harm, as evidenced by the bright glow on the targets' bodies, essentially shielding them from harm. They may also manifest Void Cells and Constructs with supportive functions to assist them in multiple treatments at once, but they will fend for themselves and those who treat them. When overwhelmed, Martyrs open a spatial rift and throw flaming objects into it, and later multiple rifts from above will emerge, showering down comet-like burning objects on their target, likewise it can harm only to their enemies while treating any friendlies within its radius. |-|Variant F= ==Seeker==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Seeker A.png|thumb|A male Seeker holding a bow and arrow.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Seeker B.png|thumb|A female Seeker wielding daggers. Her wings are more pronounced than her male counterpart.|420x420px|link=]]
Their overall designs are streamlined for speed and flight notably on part of their suits which have its aerodynamic designs particularly on their extremities. They have several apertures on lower extremities and their torso which use it as a form of propulsion. They often have tailcoats on their lower backs on their suits, others have long and flowing accessories situating on their backs. Their spinal columns have on their upper side of their thoracic region, there are two extended golden protrusions between the shoulder blades, with a faint glow apertures underneath in it. ===Equipment=== Seekers' range weapons frequently prefer something that allows their projectiles to chase after their targets to complement their overwhelming speed and properties, such as using automatic firearms or precision weapons, often modified for silent and covert operations, and may even use bows with ludicrous velocity and acceleration allowing them to fire at even longer ranges. Seekers will use lighter one-handed hand weapons such as swords, daggers which can wielding in tandem with another one-handed weapons, or for handed they use two-handed spears and staves. ===Properties=== Seekers are fast and they have the ability of flight, they will launch themselves to either run or flying with immediate acceleration, despite how fast they can easily change their directions while still maintaining their speed and velocity. As previously stated, they will use their speed to created shockwaves whether they stop or while on the run, and those who were hit by them will be blown away and some violently exploded if they are close within their path. They can also collide, causing a massive wave of energy to blast everyone in their vicinity. They create several bright star-like objects that orbit around these variants, protecting their users from incoming dangers that will intervene by launching themselves towards it, destroying them both, or they can use them to launch all of them to their targets, which the objects will chase after and explode upon contact. The objects appear to become more potent the more Seekers ran farther and faster. They distort their surroundings, and their state makes these variants destructive in small-scale attacks enhanced in their weapons and themselves, causing reality to implode due to stress. Gravity pulls anyone left behind and crushes them when they run. Seekers will expend a large amount of energy, greatly increasing their speed and lethality, because all of their other properties are more destructive, larger, and have a wider range. |-|Variant G= ==Arcane==
  • Male
  • Female
[[File:Arcane A.png|thumb|A male Arcane demonstrating of manifesting its "elemental" properties all at once.|420x420px|link=]]
[[File:Arcane B.png|thumb|A female Arcane demonstrating of manifesting its "elemental" properties all at once.|420x420px|link=]]
Donning either a robe, a cape, or some lavish accessories, and some of their helmet that somewhat similar to a wizardsThey can taken them off. However, Its function whether the part of their power source or purely for fashion aesthetics.. Much like the Martyrs, these Arcane variants' suits signifies their role, which they are compared to them as a scholar or scientists, they are noticeable on some parts of their suits have fashionable pieces of cloth or some have longer robes enough to reach down to the floor. Their spines have golden arch with dazzling space emanating in the middle situated on there backs. ===Equipment=== They are known to equip staves, or strangely, they use books to enhance their abilities, or equipped with curved swords or bladed scepters that are imbued strange energy on their edge of their blade. They do equip for ranged engagements such as rifles or bows which they imbued with their properties. ===Properties=== Arcane variants are wielding "elemental powersSuch as fire, ice, earth, and electricity but it is believed they possess more than we presume.," They use these elements more than just combat as each elements they have has wider variety of uses. Sometimes, it can "combine" their elements creating different effects whenever an Arcane desire. Strangely, they can imbue their own weapons to inflict more damage with different elements and even granting friendlies with benefits such as there is one rare case where an Arcane granted a wanderer a elemental abilities just to fend off from entities. They can create Void Cells which comprises entirely of whenever it based on its elements, they behave similar to their normal counterparts but being only different are their properties and method of attacks. If they draw more power, they will manifest this large forms based on their elements (including combined elements) such as fire tornados, large chunk of ice or blizzards, creating earthquake and etc., dealing wide-range destruction or sometimes granting benefits from their
====Unconfirmed Variants==== A few witnesses indicate that there are additional unidentified variants across the levels, they are described vaguely and are untrustworthy. More analysis and records with sufficient data are required. ==Connections and Relations== The Travelers are generally cordial toward wanderers, though their relationships with groups vary depending on their beliefs and opinions. ===Groups=== Most groups view Travelers favorably due to their reputation for saving the lives of many wanderers, making them relatively harmless. Many groups eagerly contact them when assigned to dangerous tasks. ==== [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]] ==== The M.E.G. shows interest with these entities, usually their knowledge and technology. However, they are selective in the information they share. In some instances, Travelers willingly provide some information, such as details about newly discovered levels, entities, or objects, though they require trust with these entities. They are the first group encounter of these entities. ==== [[Backroom Colonists|Backroom Colonists]] ==== They are frequent visitors to the colonies, they are welcome in their presence. The Colonists value their company which it spread stories and rumors about them, gaining favorable reputation. They have been known to provide defense and initiate rescue operations, further strengthening their relationship. ==== [[Backrooms Red Cross|Backrooms Red Cross]] ==== The Travelers and the Red Cross share a mutual belief in the preservation of life, fostering a cooperative relationship between two groups. They provide security with the group while assisting them when traveling through levels. ==== [[Recovered Data File|Marauders]] ==== The Travelers refer to the Marauders "Sleepers" but some of them calling them "Soldiers" in a respectable manner. While their stark differences between these groups are formidable, the Marauders excel in aggressive assault and the inherit immunity to anomalous effects. In contrast, the Travelers employing their quick and ambush tactics backed by their reality-warping properties. Engagements between the two groups are shared with unusual hesitance. Travelers frequently pause before delivering fatal blows while expressing distress or melancholy, while Marauders exhibit involuntary behaviors commonly twitches in their bodies, suggesting internal conflict. Notably, there are accounts of Marauders sparing Travelers and wanderers, with some even plead them to flee or even self-terminating to avoid further engagements. It is revealed that the Travelers and Marauders share a common origin, having once belonged to the same faction before diverging into two opposing ideologies. === Entities === Travelers are just as interested in encountering entities as they are curious about the Backrooms. Initially, they presumed the Backrooms were empty and inhabited only by other Outsiders until they discovered otherwise. They will engage in combat or protect wanderers from danger when necessary. Travelers may interact with entities for research purposes. Some entities, whether dead or alive, may be captured and taken to their Gate as research samples. === Other === The following sections provide insight into the Travelers' history, origins, and perspectives. ==== [[The Palace in the Void|The Palace]] ==== The Travelers have disclosed the existence of numerous Palaces scattered throughout the Backrooms and beyond, extending into other realities. Instead of level, they are megastructures, constructed by their ancestors. Most Palaces within the Backrooms are left abandoned or fell into Derelict, having fallen into disrepair due to the absence of them and/or creators. Despite their state, some remain guarded by ancestral constructs such as Keepers, leaving them as remnants. The Travelers have been exploring these structures for years, using their properties and knowledge to uncover secrets about their creators and their purpose. ==== [[The Talking Box|I.A.M.]] ==== They were originally tools utilized by both the Marauders and Travelers during their shared history. However, both of them regard as more than mere tools, treating them as loyal companions. Upon learning that an I.A.M. is in the possession of M.E.G., the Travelers expressed significant interest in retrieving it. Despite diplomatic attempts to retrieve it but it was reportedly stolen and remains missing. Its current whereabouts are unknown, though the Travelers continue to search for it. ==== Backrooms ==== The Travelers and other Outsiders refer to the Backrooms as the "Chimera" or, humorously, "Trash World." Their perspective on this reality diverges from that of wanderers. Their classification system for levels is different, featuring precise coordinates, clusters, and categories for contents. They assert that the levels and objects wanderers have documented represent only a fraction of the Backrooms' full extent. This suggests that the Travelers have traversed levels beyond wanderer discovery. The Travelers describe the Frontrooms as "safe islands in turbulent seas," implying that the Frontrooms are not separate but integral to the Backrooms. They also suggest the existence of multiple Frontrooms, lending credence to multiverse theories. Historical accounts from the Travelers link the Backrooms to a catastrophic event known as the "First Cataclysm," during which the Marauders deployed weapons capable of annihilating entire realities. This fragmentation is theorized to have created the liminal spaces now identified as the Backrooms. Unlike other fragmented spaces, the Backrooms are unique due to their continuous and seemingly infinite expansion. ==== Outsiders ==== Outsiders, like them, are beings that capable of transcending the boundaries of their respective realities. They encompass a wide variety of civilizations, species, and individuals. Travelers have reportedly encountered Outsiders in the past, both as allies and adversaries, suggesting the existence of other factions beyond the Travelers and Marauders. Future encounters with Outsiders are inevitable, necessitating caution and preparedness during such interactions. ==== The Outside ==== The Outside is not considered a reality, nor an absence like the Void, nor in between reality. (It is difficult to explain or may take considerable time to understand, please see Interviews for more details) ==== Predecessors ==== Their ancestors. Interestingly, they were supposed to be an extinct race but how exactly they were brought back but they explained they were brought back by the Soldiers (before they became the Marauders). It is still yet known of how they maintain their numbers nowadays, they did mention about this of "Genesis" though what exactly this is still unknown. But other of their members from the Outside are currently searching for survivors and join up with them. ==Encounter== It would be unwise to confront Travelers due to their adept in combat coupled with their immense reality-warping abilities. Though they prefer not to engage with lethal force as much as possible, they will attempt to subdue instead. However, when employing a Traveler, the wanderer must be aware that they are not interested in any favors, especially for dubious reasons. == Discovery== [[File:Traveler Encounter.jpg|thumb|A picture is taken when an injured wanderer pursued by entities only to be intervened by this lone Traveler.|400x400px|link=]] The first documented encounter with a Traveler occurred when a group of wanderers and are en route from [[Level 0|Level 0]] to [[Level 1|Level 1]] for surveying but they were attacked by hostile entities. This resulted in fatalities and the separation of several wanderers and cause them to separate. The lone wanderer found themselves surrounded by entities. Until a Traveler intervened, eliminating the entities that pursue the wanderer. Afterward, the Traveler assisted the wanderer to return back into their outpost to safety. They eventually reached Level 1, where they encountered a nearby outpost of the M.E.G. The arrival of the Traveler in their outpost sparked a stir among the wanderers at the outpost, who gathered around to observe the entity that had rescued. The Traveler left from their shortly thereafter. This incident led to these rumors surrounding the "Man in a White Suit." It met with skepticism, but the rumors gained more traction as more wanderers reported several sightings of Travelers in several levels, who were seen rescuing wanderers and eliminating entities across various levels. Following these reports, several groups have began investigations, eventually confirming the existence of the Travelers. Their group's representative, known as the Elder, later introduced itself to the groups. After some time, encounters with Travelers have become frequent, and often seen accompanying wanderers, providing protection, and offering assistance as needed. ==Additional Information== === Individual of Interest: Elder === [[File:Doc.jpg|thumb|Doc visiting to a Colonist base and met some wanderers along way, waving to greet them as he passing by.|280x280px|link=]] An individual among the Travelers, known as the "Elder" or referred to it as "Doc" by wanderers. It is a Martyr variant and is recognized for its kindness and willingness to treat and assist wanderers and settlements whenever possible. The Elder is a mysterious figure in their group. Their common and acceptable rumor that the Elder is the last surviving member of the Predecessors. According to these rumor, the Elder was the sole survivor during the extinction of their race. Whenever they interview Elder, it chooses to keep its real name and personal history private, the reasons for this is remained unknown. Despite this, it has intimate knowledge of the Predecessors and the Palace, which has greatly contributed to the Travelers' research efforts. The Elder is the one of their members that preserving most of their culture and history of their people. As the figurehead of their group, the Elder is responsible fostering relationships with other groups. To this end, they frequently visit various factions personally, working to establish friendly relations and alliances whenever possible. === Interviews === There are few members who have enough time for themselves to be interview though it requires some private areas to share their information, regarding their backgrounds, histories, and etc.
==== Interview T-001 (Person of Interest: Elder) ==== Given the fact that he is one of their surviving Predecessor, he hold vast knowledge of their history, the Hallowed Palace, and the Marauders and their events. Elder is cooperative, however, he refuses to open about his background and his identity for reasons.
'''Open >>'''
'''Close <<'''
'''Interviewer:''' Brandon Hill '''Interviewee:''' Elder (Doc) '''Foreword:''' While the Red Cross arrives in [[Level -599]] and stay for a month, for some days, Doc and other few Travelers arrives to provide assistance for the groups. Doc was in a medical tending wounded wanderers using with his healing properties, an agent approach Doc for an interview regarding their arrivals and history, and perhaps, their seemingly rivalry with the Marauders. [Audio-to-Text transcripts] '''''' '''Brandon Hill:''' Hey Doc! '''Doc:''' Oh hello, Mr. Hill, um—why do you call me... "Doc?" '''Brandon Hill:''' A lot of people call you that, and I think the name fits you. '''Doc:''' If they insist on calling me such, then I have nothing against it. But Mr. Hill, is there something you wish to discuss with me? '''Brandon Hill:''' Yeah, they're interested about you guys. So... can you tell us about your race? If you wanted to. '''Doc:''' Very well. We are a race whose names hold no importance. Yet they have called us by many: the Golden Lords, Starborne, and more. To you, you have called us simply the Travelers. We are explorers. Curiosity is our nature, to seek beyond worlds and stars. '''Brandon Hill:''' I did recall some of you guys saying you were supposedly extinct until you were brought back alive. What happened? '''Doc:''' The First Cataclysm. '''Brandon Hill:''' I’ve heard about that before... Weren't you and the Marauders once teamed up and finished it off? '''Doc:''' Yes. But it was before it happened. '''Brandon Hill:''' Okay. Then what's this "First Cataclysm?" '''Doc:''' Nameless things... Abominations, a perversion of everything... Even a corruption corrupted by it. '''Brandon Hill:''' Uh... okay? If it was before all that happened, then how did it all start? '''Doc:''' It remains uncertain. No one knows where it came from, not even us. I do recall when my kind and I lived a prosperous life, journeying far and wide until that fragment appeared and plagued that unfortunate world. '''Brandon Hill:''' Fragment? '''Doc:''' An anomaly, a stone of pure alien darkness that can turn unfortunate souls into these... resentful, agonizing husks. We did not know it was already spreading in different realities like an ever-consuming fire on a grass field. We tried to cure and quell the infection with our powers, but it did not work. Instead, they mutated into these abominations. For our kindness, naivety, and complacency, my people were slaughtered, and I was the sole survivor... except me. [Doc becomes quiet as if he is contemplating, with a melancholic body gesture.] '''Doc:''' I was there to witness this gruesome scenery, to see my people selflessly try to save them, only to return their favor in bloodshed on the pristine floors of our Termini. As I fell into slumber for eternity, I thought I would wake up in a dark derelict—alone in the darkness—until the Soldiers came in and rescued me. '''Brandon Hill:''' Oh. It must've been hard for you... I'm sorry to hear that. '''Doc:''' There is no need for pity... Are there more questions you want to ask? '''Brandon Hill:''' It may sound insensitive of me to say, but wouldn’t that infecting your people too? '''Doc:''' No. We cannot turn into those things, fortunately. But seeing other alien races turning into that? I shudder at the thought of being prisoners in their own bodies, forever tortured as they slowly fade away from who they are, becoming wretched souls who beg to die, but the thing will not let them—letting them scream in agony and hate. '''Brandon Hill:''' And about the Marauders—who are they exactly? '''Doc:''' A formidable army whose purpose is the total eradication of the First Cataclysm by any means necessary. Yet beneath their ruthlessness and indifference lies their selflessness and aspiration to bring hope to those struck by the First Cataclysm. They are a race of lowly copies, whose visage is as empty as a white canvas, mind you. Yet they are the only ones with enough nerve to face against it—they gave their expendable lives to save many. '''Brandon Hill:''' And that's where they found you and woke you up, right? '''Doc:''' Indeed. At first, I thought they would capture me as scavengers for my people’s crafts for value, using me as a living trophy. But the Soldiers brought me to their place, treating me from my awakening with such care and hospitality. After that, I met one of the five Founders. '''Brandon Hill:''' Founders? I never thought they had leaders. '''Doc:''' They are... somewhat, but they insist they do not have any form of leadership, only a strong bond and brotherhood. '''Brandon Hill:''' And who could that be? '''Doc:''' He is Adam, the Founder of the "Liberator" Division. Though the Soldiers' bland uniforms and drab colors are unimpressive, he gave such an authoritative presence in a small room. He told me they knew my people’s traces through some of their men in their daring excursions until they found me. '''Brandon Hill:''' Why did he say all of that? '''Doc:''' He ensured me that I was safe in their hands, and they provided me with a place to stay. Though, he did make a simple request, in return for saving me. '''Brandon Hill:''' And what’s that? '''Doc:''' My knowledge, about the First Cataclysm. He told me everything that had happened. As the thing spread rampant while they were at war with it during my slumber. They wanted my knowledge of how to eradicate the thing. They saw me as the key to end the nightmare, and so, I obliged to share what I had learned. It was the least I could do to assist them in their war, but my knowledge alone was not enough. '''Brandon Hill:''' And that’s where you come in. [Traveler: Hey, Elder, we're done helping these guys!] '''Doc:''' Sorry, Mr. Hill, it is our time to depart. You can continue this conversation through one of my people, perhaps Castle will share our information. '''Brandon Hill:''' Okay. Thanks for helping these wanderers. '''Doc:''' Your appreciation is welcomed. I take my leave. ''''''
==== Interview T-002 (Castle) ==== Castle is an Architect who specializes study in "Euclidean Onto-Geometries" and "Xeno-Technologies," it is known of being a laidback entity and generally getting along with other wanderers and Travelers alike. It is well-acquainted with Elder. In regards of theirs, as a group, and how they came to be, along with the Marauders, Outsiders, and the Backrooms itself.
'''Open >>'''
'''Close <<'''
'''Interviewer:''' Brandon Hill '''Interviewee:''' Castle '''Foreword:''' Brandon Hill and other wanderers are trekking through [[Level 37]] for their routine check. They were exploring into deeper areas in this level until they spotted a Traveler, doing something alone. This prompted to began an interview. [Audio-to-Text transcripts] '''''' '''Castle:''' Hey. The name's Castle, are you Brandon? [A harmonic sound is produced while manifesting a marble structure.] '''Brandon Hill:''' Yeah? Did he tell you about it? '''Castle:''' Yeah, I thought I'd meet you someday. '''Brandon Hill:''' What're you doing here alone? '''Castle:''' Just checking the weird stuff in this part of the sub-sector. You? '''Brandon Hill:''' Same thing. '''Castle:''' Came here just to have a chat with me? '''Brandon Hill:''' Supposed so, you're busy? '''Castle:''' Nah, I'm done here. I was supposed to walk around and probably look for interesting stuff, but I guess I'm getting an interview instead. So, ask away. '''Brandon Hill:''' Alright. I wanted to ask Doc about how you guys came since he's alone, right? '''Castle:''' Chambers of Genesis. '''Brandon Hill:''' What's that? '''Castle:''' It's the thing that made us poof into existence. I'm one of those inside those things. Well... we were supposed to be open during Elder's time, but y'know, it all went to shit and we met with empty derelicts and a bunch of Soldiers with guns at the ready instead. '''Brandon Hill:''' How many of you woke up? '''Castle:''' Initially, about fifteen or so. But as time passed and we looked for more Chambers of Genesis in some barely functioning Derelicts? We grew to about dozens of thousands. '''Brandon Hill:''' Next question. When did you guys join with the Marauders? Before they all became what they are now. '''Castle:''' Marauders—you mean the Sleepers? I think you mean Soldiers. '''Brandon Hill:''' Okay, so how did you join with the Soldiers, then? '''Castle:''' To make it short: those guys can be a bunch of really hard-asses because they're against the whole enlisting "civilians" to their cause, even though we have powers that could level a star, which we didn't know we had in us. So, in their tradition, to prove them wrong, we got their incomplete design, which is the suits they're wearing, and that's how the Matrix was made. Though, they did train us with one of their best so we could fight while wearing it, for field testing, of course. '''Brandon Hill:''' That's surprising... and I can see the similarities between the two of you. Did they give you guys a pass? '''Castle:''' No, not yet. Though they did send us on small missions dealing with criminals and others first. Then, later down the line, we had to deal with dangerous anomalies and bigger baddies until we got used to it. Thinking about it, they were being careful and showing us how they do their job. '''Brandon Hill:''' And that's the part where you guys finally got into a real fight? '''Castle:''' Yeah... A lot of them died, and some of us weren't the same... [Castle pauses for a moment.] '''Brandon Hill:''' Not want to talk about it? '''Castle:''' Yeah... '''Brandon Hill:''' Okay. Then what about the Outside, what is it exactly? '''Castle:''' Let's compare this "Backrooms" to a building, right? It has all the rooms inside it and whatnot, and every building has its own set of rules that make it normal and has its own barriers to protect itself from, well... Outside, except this one. This place is like a run-down building using garbage to hold it all together. Outside, like what it's called, is practically empty, a free-for-all place. '''Brandon Hill:''' [Pause] Like the Void? '''Castle:''' Hmm... Not quite. It's basically a shape that can mold where all of it can be put on or taken off. '''Brandon Hill:''' You guys know a lot of stuff. '''Castle:''' Well, learning stuff is our hobby and that's my job too. '''Brandon Hill:''' Since you're one of those Outsiders, what are you guys exactly? '''Castle:''' Well, we're Outsiders, obviously. If you mean how we get into this place, it's not the same as you guys getting into this place. But there's a caveat though, since we're an Outsider it could get strange. '''Brandon Hill:''' How? '''Castle:''' Because we don't belong in any realities unless an Outsider is unprepared, but that's not an issue for us anyway. '''Brandon Hill:''' You think there are other Outsiders living in the Backrooms? '''Castle:''' I'm guessing there would be pirates and other scavengers probably looking for a place to hide or make a quick buck by scrounging up in this place. But I doubt they landed near you guys, so I think you're all safe, aside from the Sleepers though. '''Brandon Hill:''' Since you mentioned the ability to go out of the Backrooms whenever you want, can't you guys just bring us home to the Frontrooms? '''Castle:''' It's complicated. '''Brandon Hill:''' Why? '''Castle:''' Well... that "Frontrooms" you mentioned is basically a part of Chimera and then there's a bunch of them scattered all over the place. Kinda tangled up to be honest. '''Brandon Hill:''' [Pauses] You mean... there's more than one? '''Castle:''' Yeah... And it's all came from that yellow maze, a lot of holes apparently. '''Brandon Hill:''' Still, you're the Travelers, I'm sure you can do it with all of that godly power. '''Castle:''' Like I said, it's complicated. Think of the Frontrooms as safe islands in the middle of a stormy ocean. '''Brandon Hill:''' But, you guys can fix it, right? I heard you guys can do that on some levels into safer ones. '''Castle:''' Look, man. You're asking us to sort out colored grains of sand in the right place while fixing it. '''Brandon Hill:''' So what can we do? '''Castle:''' There's nothing you can do now but wait, unfortunately. Sorry to disappoint you. '''Brandon Hill:''' [sighs] Well, at least you're helping us. I think we're done here, we're heading back. What about you? '''Castle:''' Eh... I wanna walk some more, probably to the deeper parts of this place. You guys go ahead. '''Brandon Hill:''' Okay, be safe out there. '''Castle:''' You too, man. ''''''
